title: The Food Wife

jokes.text: And here's a marble for Lisa for cleaning her room, and a marble for Bart for not trashing Lisa's room.
jokes.setup: Rewarding children with marbles for good behavior
jokes.punchline: Rewarding Bart specifically for NOT doing something destructive
jokes.explanation: Cleverly establishes Bart's destructive nature while showing parental desperation - rewarding non-destruction as if it's an achievement

jokes.text: This Saturday, from the dad who brought you cemetery paintball and go-karts on real roads, comes the greatest activity yet... Video game convention!
jokes.punchline: Anti-climactic reveal of a tame video game convention
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure with Homer's dangerous parenting history, but the punchline is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Is there any better feeling than cutting in line because a plastic badge says you're special?
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of the shallow pleasure of perceived status
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Homer's oblivious honesty - relatable and quotable

jokes.text: GTS! So many games. W.O.K.?! Ooh, the Flying Tomato. Ah! TMTOLO! DL2L-to-D! Oh! ACSOL! HC1?! Whoa! Guts of War Two: Entrails of Intestinox!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous video game acronyms and over-the-top violent titles
jokes.explanation: Good parody of gaming culture's obsession with acronyms and violence, but runs a bit long

jokes.text: Colon slash! Rectum kill!
jokes.punchline: Anatomically specific and crude combat moves
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that fits the video game parody but relies on shock value over cleverness

jokes.text: We've made a game that'll reward the hardcore gamer with hundreds and hundreds of hours of... - Finished it. - Huh?
jokes.setup: Developer boasting about game's epic length
jokes.punchline: Bart instantly completing it mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and structure - perfectly captures both gaming culture and Bart's video game prowess

jokes.text: But working on this game cost me my marriage. I-I have twins I've never met! - Well, when you meet them, tell them your game's too easy.
jokes.setup: Developer's heartbreaking confession about personal sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Bart's callous dismissal focused only on game difficulty
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor that perfectly captures Bart's self-centered nature and gaming culture's obsession with difficulty over human cost

jokes.text: From the precision footwork of Ohio State to the lame, dead-on-arrival humor of Stanford.
jokes.setup: Describing college marching bands in the game
jokes.punchline: Brutal roast of Stanford's comedy reputation
jokes.explanation: Specific cultural reference that works as insider knowledge but doesn't integrate with character

jokes.text: Uh-oh, I'm losing the crowd!
jokes.punchline: Developer's panicked realization about his boring presentation
jokes.explanation: Standard conference presentation humor - functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Level cleared. Next level: study for chemistry test on 12-hour bus ride home.
jokes.setup: Lisa playing a marching band game
jokes.punchline: Game mechanics applied to Lisa's studious real life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa joke - transforms her academic obsession into gaming terms seamlessly

jokes.text: This is the funnest game ever! Man, how many quarters does this game take?
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying a game at the convention
jokes.punchline: He doesn't understand it's a modern gaming convention, not an arcade
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization showing his disconnect from modern gaming culture

jokes.text: Out of my way! Move it! Geeks must yield to normals!
jokes.punchline: Declaring social hierarchy at a geek convention while being there himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious hypocrisy and social insensitivity perfectly captured in one line

jokes.text: Room's full. Hmm. Aha! [climbing through vents] Yay! Woo-hoo!
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of him crawling through air vents like an action hero
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer physical comedy - predictable but effective visual humor

jokes.text: Conventions rock! Fun Dad rocks! I need a nap!
jokes.punchline: Immediate crash after the adrenaline high
jokes.explanation: Good energy crash joke that shows Homer's limited stamina despite enthusiasm

jokes.text: Head up to the tub while I deflate your toys. Save us, Fun Dad!
jokes.punchline: Homer literally deflating toys, kids treating him like a superhero
jokes.explanation: Sweet family moment with mild visual humor from the toy deflation detail

jokes.text: Yeah, I was on today. Scary on.
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware but slightly concerning pride
jokes.explanation: Homer's slightly unsettling self-awareness about his intensity as a parent

jokes.text: Look, honey, a family's like a team. And on every team, you have the slam-dunking megastar and the referee.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining family dynamics with sports metaphor
jokes.punchline: Casting Marge as the unfun referee while he's the star
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that reveals Homer's selfish view of parenting roles

jokes.text: Moms want to be fun, but we're stuck with all the mom stuff.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing frustration about parenting roles
jokes.punchline: The inherent contradiction of responsible parenting vs. being fun
jokes.explanation: Relatable parenting humor that captures Marge's eternal struggle

jokes.text: What if... we roll pennies and go to the dollar store? - That's good, Marge! Get all the terrible ideas out of your system.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting a modest fun activity
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutal honesty about how terrible the idea is
jokes.explanation: Homer's encouraging but completely insulting response perfectly captures their dynamic

jokes.text: Your Saturday Surprise Mom Day is the X-Games! [Cut to] Welcome to the Cross Games, Simpsons. A Christian fellowship expo!
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal of Christian fellowship event with cross logo
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect visual punchline - the logo switch is brilliant

jokes.text: Are there at least games here? - Oh, no, 'games' stands for Gathering of American Messengers for Evangelical Sports.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous acronym that eliminates any fun
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym humor that builds on the religious bait-and-switch

jokes.text: Sports? - 'Sports' stands for Strict Parental Oversight Rather Than Sports.
jokes.punchline: Even more restrictive acronym that negates itself
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation that doubles down on the acronym gag with self-negating logic

jokes.text: Come on, kids, fun comes from inside. It isn't about what we actually do. - Yes, it is! That's all it is!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to spin the disappointment philosophically
jokes.punchline: Kids' blunt rejection of her forced positivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of parental platitudes - kids cut through the BS with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Two children to play debtors in the Parable of the Unjust Steward. All the other kids are at something called the X-Games.
jokes.punchline: Oblivious mention that all other kids are at the real X-Games
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony but doesn't integrate well with main characters

jokes.text: I hate it when grown-ups call kids 'gang.'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's specific complaint about the word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa observation - articulate criticism of adult condescension

jokes.text: Why do old squirrels always crawl into my engine to die?
jokes.punchline: Homer's weirdly specific and morbid explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Homer logic - specific and disturbing enough to be memorable

jokes.text: We're not a gang! Gangs are cool!
jokes.setup: Homer calling them a gang again
jokes.punchline: Bart's clarification that gangs are actually cool
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds Bart's perspective - gangs aren't offensive, they're aspirational

jokes.text: That newborn has earrings!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's shocked observation about infant piercing
jokes.explanation: Standard culture shock humor - works for character but not particularly original

jokes.text: They're using pancakes as spoons. Ooh, let's see what else they do wrong.
jokes.punchline: Bart's assumption that different equals wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Bart's cultural ignorance and troublemaking nature

jokes.text: They're selling CDs in the restaurant! Back to the car!
jokes.setup: Marge continuing to find reasons to leave
jokes.punchline: CD sales as the final straw for her comfort zone
jokes.explanation: Shows Marge's cultural anxiety but the observation itself isn't that funny

jokes.text: Oh, I'll just have a side salad. - We have no side salad. - Back to the car!
jokes.setup: Marge ordering the safest possible item
jokes.punchline: Even that's not available, triggering her retreat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Marge's cultural anxiety - side salad as ultimate safe choice

jokes.text: Okay, Marge, you test-drove a convertible once; you can do this.
jokes.setup: Marge psyching herself up to try ethnic food
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to her most daring life experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant characterization - shows how limited Marge's 'adventurous' experiences are

jokes.text: Holy casserole-y! That's good gloop!
jokes.punchline: Her wholesome exclamation and description
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge-ism - substituting wholesome words for actual cursing

jokes.text: I wish I lived in Ethiopia!
jokes.punchline: Innocent wish that ignores Ethiopia's serious problems
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through Marge's naivety, but treads close to insensitive territory

jokes.text: Exotic, getarian, I can mention it in a college essay.
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking about how to leverage it academically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa joke - can't enjoy anything without calculating its academic value

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait, what is she eating? They've never served me that dish, and I wear indigenous beaded headgear.
jokes.setup: Foodie noticing Marge got special treatment
jokes.punchline: Pointing out their cultural costume as qualification
jokes.explanation: Good satire of foodie cultural appropriation and entitlement

jokes.text: They have prepared her a dish from the non-translated page!
jokes.setup: Building on the special dish discovery
jokes.punchline: Dramatic revelation about menu exclusivity
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that satirizes foodie obsession with authenticity and exclusivity

jokes.text: So, did all of your cars break down? - Mom, they're here on purpose. They're foodies.
jokes.setup: Homer's logical assumption about why people are there
jokes.punchline: Revelation that people choose to be in this neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - can't fathom voluntary adventure in uncomfortable circumstances

jokes.text: We discovered Korean barbeque in this town. - Uh, before the Koreans? - Oh, sure they cook it, but they don't get it.
jokes.setup: Foodie claiming discovery of Korean BBQ
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim that Koreans don't understand their own food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of foodie cultural appropriation and arrogance - perfectly captures the absurdity

jokes.text: Oh, no. I don't eat anything new unless I've eaten it before.
jokes.setup: Family trying to get Homer to try new food
jokes.punchline: Homer's logically impossible food philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely circular reasoning delivered with total conviction. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Marge, the kids are acting ethnic! Relax, Homie. Have some leftover Galalalalalalalalah.
jokes.setup: Homer's alarm at children's cultural engagement
jokes.punchline: Marge's dismissive attitude and impossible-to-pronounce dish name
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with Homer's cultural anxiety and Marge's new foodie confidence. The dish name is effectively absurd.

jokes.text: You're already a fatty and a drunkie.
jokes.setup: Bart encouraging Homer to be a foodie
jokes.punchline: Listing his existing food-related problems
jokes.explanation: Good Bart sass but relatively straightforward insult humor

jokes.text: Oh, and here's my favorite restaurant: La Fridge. It's open 24 hours a day and there's no dress code.
jokes.punchline: Treating his refrigerator as a French restaurant
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Homer's laziness and food attitude

jokes.text: The Three Mouthketeers. - Three? You mean, one, two... me?
jokes.punchline: Homer's sad realization he's excluded
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's exclusion arc, but the math joke is predictable

jokes.text: All right, food nerds, reality check. All the food in those pictures is poop by now. Minds blown, you're welcome.
jokes.punchline: Crude but technically accurate observation about digestion
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflation of pretentiousness with gross but logical observation

jokes.text: My kids do not eat sorbet; they eat sherbet. And they pronounce it 'sherbert,' and they wish it was ice cream.
jokes.punchline: Homer's detailed defense of working-class food preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent class comedy - perfectly captures Homer's defensive blue-collar authenticity

jokes.text: Fine, blow off Fun Dad and go eat your walrus mustaches and deep-fried pixie wangs.
jokes.setup: Homer's angry reaction to being excluded
jokes.punchline: Making up ridiculous fancy food names
jokes.explanation: Good angry Homer rant with creative made-up food items

jokes.text: Aah! Jealous? Me? That is to laugh. The very idea. If anything... you're the one who's jealous. Of what? Of how much fun I am to be around!
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly desperate denial and deflection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's insecurity and poor deflection skills

jokes.text: When I'm sad, I make baseball bats. - When I'm happy, I make birdhouses.
jokes.punchline: Contrasting his violent vs. nurturing emotional outlets
jokes.explanation: Clever insight into Homer's psychology through his crafting choices

jokes.text: I'm food bad boy Tony Bourdain. There's nowhere I won't go and nothing I won't eat, as long as I'm paid in emeralds and my hotel room has a bidet that shoots warm champagne.
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated diva demands despite 'adventurous' image
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity parody contrasting public persona with private demands

jokes.text: Stop your bloody whinging, Marge. You (bleep) it up, big blue, didn't you? Why did you invite Homer? He stole your bloody thunder.
jokes.punchline: Gordon Ramsay appearing to berate her in her own dream
jokes.explanation: Creative dream sequence with accurate Gordon Ramsay parody voice

jokes.text: But there weren't four Musketeers. - Yeah-huh. Athos, Porthos, Aramis and D'Artagnan. - D'Artagnan wasn't a Musketeer. He only had a letter of introduction to the captain of the guards-- which he lost!
jokes.setup: Argument about the number of Musketeers
jokes.punchline: Lisa's detailed literary correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - can't resist showing off literary knowledge even in casual conversation

jokes.text: Well, actually, I've come around on hipsters. Takes a lot of guts to all wear the same hat.
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards understanding of conformity as courage
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion - Homer misunderstanding hipster individualism as brave conformity

jokes.text: Is this the chemical kitchen place where you do the crazy cooking? - Quiet, man. The experience begins.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for the molecular gastronomy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Accidentally finding a meth lab
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - molecular gastronomy vocabulary perfectly describes meth cooking

jokes.text: The next course is Regret.
jokes.punchline: Serving an emotion as food
jokes.explanation: Good molecular gastronomy parody - abstract concepts as pretentious dishes

jokes.text: Pick up the meth! Pick up the meth! This better be dinner theater!
jokes.setup: Police raid on the meth lab
jokes.punchline: Homer still thinking it's elaborate restaurant performance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misdirection - Homer's continued confusion is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Marge! This isn't a food restaurant! It's a meth restaurant! A meth-taurant! Aah!
jokes.setup: Homer finally realizing where he is
jokes.punchline: Creating a portmanteau while panicking
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that maintains the restaurant theme while revealing the truth

jokes.text: They say you can't even understand parsnips until you had zero-g parsnips.
jokes.setup: Foodies refusing to leave fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous claim about space-cooked vegetables
jokes.explanation: Good foodie pretension parody - absurd claims about revolutionary cooking techniques

jokes.text: We get the family dressed up to go to our favorite meth lab, and it's all raided out.
jokes.setup: Criminal family arriving at raided location
jokes.punchline: Treating meth lab like a family restaurant
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating criminal enterprise with family restaurant disappointment

jokes.text: Eat deconstructed apple pie! Merci, Maman, pour la tarte tatin à la mode.
jokes.punchline: Suddenly speaking fluent French while food-fighting
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation but the French feels random rather than earned

jokes.text: Here's 50 bucks. For the next two hours, I don't want to know you exist.
jokes.punchline: Homer paying to avoid family time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ending - even in family bonding, he finds a way to be selfish

tmdb_overview: Marge, Bart and Lisa start their own food blog and land a reservation at a popular restaurant; when Marge sends Homer to a cooking lab, he finds himself in a situation with law enforcement.
